segments.text: Hi, I'm Isaac from Lovable and this is a
quick tutorial on Lovable. For some
context, Lovable is a platform which
allows you to build websites or apps
simply just by talking with AI. You
simply just describe what you want in
plain English and then the AI will
create it for you. No coding needed at
all. And then you can publish the app or
the website immediately to the web. And
people are already using Laval to build
all sorts of things from small side
projects to full software companies.
Okay, but now I'm just going to jump
straight into building our first app. So
to start off, we have this prompt window
right here. And to build your app in
Lowable, you have to start off by
writing the initial prompts. So I have
prepared this prompt right here. And
this is an example of how one of these
initial prompts could look like. So
build a platform called Fitcore that
teaches you about health and fitness. It
should have the following pages. Landing
page with a sticky navigation bar
linking to all the core sections.
Workout library page with exercise
categories. nutrition guide page with
structures, beginner friendly articles,
and a resources blog page with
card-based article previews. And then
down here, I've included some style
guides. So, we have a modern and clean
design with generous white spacing. We
have some colors I've selected, and
these are in hex format, and you can,
you know, find your colors yourself and
just make them into hex to be more
precise to loable. And then we have some
other styling guides here. And we also
have the target audience which is also
something you can include in your
initial prompt. So once we're finished
with our prompt and we can even prepare
this in a Google doc or we can chat with
chatbt to try to figure out how the
prompt should look like. When we're
happy we can just press on submit and
now loable will begin generating the
first version of our app. Okay, now the
first version of our app is finished. We
can see that we have this little preview
button right here. And we're on the
preview right now. And we're on the
homepage and we can see the hero page
right here with an image and a nice
little fade effect. And we got some some
sections here. Let's go to workouts.
Here we have our our various workouts.
It seems like some nutrition
fundamentals. And then we also have
resources right here with various
articles. Now, I don't think we will be
able to press these articles, but I'm
going to give it a try. And as I'm doing
it, nothing happens. So, this feature
hasn't been added yet. However, we can
just send another prompt to lovable and
add these articles to the website. Okay,
so I prepared a prompt and this is the
prompt. Right now, when I press the read
article button on one of the articles on
the resources page, nothing happens.
This is not the intended behavior.
Instead, we want to be taken to the
respective article page. Can you please
make dynamic pages for each article
under /blog or article that we can
navigate to when we press the cards or
the read article button? So as you guys
can see we have this little navigation
bar right here inside of Lowable itself
where we can see kind of the page
structure of our app and what we want is
that we want these articles to be
dynamic such that when you press on them
you get taken to a slash article or blog
page and under that blog page you will
have the individual articles. So that is
kind of what I've described here. So now
I'm just going to press on submit update
the website and add this feature. Now,
while making these changes such as
adding the footer or adding the blog
page, it's important that Lovable still
follows the style guides of your
website, so everything looks on brand.
It can also be important that Lovable
understands the purpose of the website
and what features you want to have in
the website during the entire building
process because if you're adding a new
feature which kind of collides with
another feature, sometimes that can
confuse Lowable and in those cases, it's
really really good to have set up custom
knowledge. So what custom knowledge
allows you to do is basically to paste
in information inside of Lavable's
context window that Lavable will
remember throughout the entire building
process. So what we can do here is that
we can actually just take our initial
prompt right here and we can copy it and
then we can go up here going into
settings and then inside of knowledge
here we can paste in all of our style
guides and all of the features that we
want on the website and maybe also the
target audience such that loable will
remember that throughout building the
entire application. And the loable AI
which is building your app as with any
other AI has a limited memory which
means over time as you prompt loable
will begin forgetting previous messages.
Of course loable will still be able to
remember you know your last messages but
if a message is really really far back
Lavable might not actually remember it.
However, we do have systems in place to
try to minimize this from occurring. But
it's still good and it's highly
recommended to describe all of the
features style guides within this kind
of custom knowledge file. Now sometimes
you may want to make subtle changes to
your website. Then it can be annoying to

and then you can actually adjust the
